name: Frontal Lobe
location: Anterior portion of cerebral cortex lying behind the forehead
description: The frontal lobe is the executive headquarters of the brain, directing working memory, abstract reasoning, decision making, social conduct, and voluntary motor output.
functions: Executive cognitive planning and working memory
Voluntary motor movement execution (Primary Motor Cortex M1)
bloodSupply: Anterior Cerebral Artery (ACA) & Middle Cerebral Artery (MCA)
clinicalImportance: Essential for personality and executive function; lesions result in disinhibition, apathy, and motor deficits.
researchNotes: fMRI studies demonstrate frontoparietal central executive network (CEN) co-activation during complex problem solving.
interestingFacts: The prefrontal cortex is the last brain region to fully mature (around age 25).
